design_philosophy.core_loop: First-person dungeon navigation with turn-based battles; recruit demons through negotiation, fuse them to grow stronger, manage alignment to unlock story branches and demon recruitment paths
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Moral choice system (Law-Neutral-Chaos, Light-Neutral-Dark axes) that gates access to areas, demons, and story outcomes; resource scarcity (magnetite for demon summoning) creates strategic summoning decisions
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Demon summoner and trainer commanding a customized party; player agency in defining moral worldview and faction allegiance; post-apocalyptic world explorer navigating Tokyo ravaged by demon invasion
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Alignment system is mechanically enforced—choices permanently alter protagonist's faction standing, locking out demons and locations; demons are negotiable NPCs rather than mere combat encounters; fusion is the only power progression path for demons, forcing active team curation; post-apocalyptic setting with warring ideological cults (Order of Messiah vs Ring of Gaia) positions player as moral arbiter

difficulty.checkpointing: Special Terminals scattered across world map allow save anywhere; teleportation between terminals available
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Alignment choices affect demon recruitment and availability, creating emergent difficulty spikes if player locks themselves out of powerful demon types; demon fusion system allows soft power progression tuning; resource management (magnetite drain) creates tactical summoning constraints

progression.upgrade_system: Demon fusion system (2-3 demons fuse into single more powerful demon) as exclusive demon power progression; human party members gain levels through combat and randomly learn new abilities; weapon/item purchases from scattered merchants
progression.unlock_mechanism: Alignment gates determine area access (Law players blocked from Chaos locations, etc.); story branches based on moral decisions; demon recruitment locked by protagonist-demon alignment compatibility
replayability_hooks: Three primary faction paths (Law/Messiah, Neutral, Chaos/Gaia) with different story progression
Nine-axis alignment system (3×3 Law-Neutral-Chaos and Light-Neutral-Dark combinations) creates varied demon recruitment options per playthrough
Different demons recruitable based on alignment, incentivizing alternate moral stances
Area gating by alignment creates distinct zone exploration patterns

world_building_techniques: Near-future Tokyo setting (199X) transitioning to post-apocalyptic ruined Tokyo 30 years later
Two opposing ideological cults (Order of Messiah/Yahweh vs Ring of Gaia/Lucifer) representing Law and Chaos moral extremes
Demon invasion as setting-wide catastrophe establishing stakes
Moral choice narrative structure where player's decisions reshape faction relationships and available story content
Character designs by Kazuma Kaneko incorporating mythological figures and cultural clothing styles

faction_indicators: Order of Messiah (Law-aligned, Yahweh cult) blocks Chaos-aligned players from churches
Ring of Gaia (Chaos-aligned, Lucifer cult) represents opposing ideological extreme
Alignment system (9-point grid) publicly signals moral stance through demon party composition and accessible zones
danger_signals: Nuclear strike on Tokyo by US military (early plot event) establishes geopolitical danger
Demon invasion aftermath creates post-apocalyptic danger environment
Resource scarcity (magnetite depletion) creates survival pressure
environmental_storytelling: Post-apocalyptic Tokyo ruins document civilization collapse
Cathedrals of Shadows (demon fusion sites) as supernatural infrastructure
Merchant hubs and special terminals scattered across world map indicate remnant human civilization pockets
Faction-controlled areas (Law vs Chaos churches) visually segregate ideological space
